title: Project Manager
description:
We are seeking an experienced and highly organized Project Manager to lead and manage ship design and engineering projects from concept through to delivery. As a core member of our team, you will be responsible for planning, executing, and delivering ship design projects on time, within scope, and within budget. You will liaise closely with clients, classification societies, subcontractors, and internal multidisciplinary teams to ensure successful outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead and manage end-to-end ship design projects including concept, basic, and detailed design stages.
  • Develop and maintain comprehensive project plans including scope, schedules, budgets, deliverables, and resource allocations.
  • Act as the primary point of contact for clients and external stakeholders, ensuring consistent communication and updates.
  • Coordinate and facilitate internal design and engineering teams (e.g., naval architecture, structural, piping, electrical).
  • Identify risks and issues, proactively develop mitigation strategies, and resolve problems in a timely manner.
  • Ensure that all work complies with project requirements, relevant standards, classification rules, and regulations.
  • Conduct regular project reviews and prepare progress reports for internal and external stakeholders.
  • Monitor project performance using appropriate systems, tools, and KPIs.
  • Support contract and change order management, billing schedules, and commercial negotiations where required.
  • Drive continuous improvement in project execution processes and cross-functional collaboration.

Requirements

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Naval Architecture, Marine Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or a related discipline.
  • PMP certification or equivalent project management training is an advantage.

Experience:

  • Minimum 5–8 years of relevant experience in ship design, engineering, or shipbuilding projects.
  • Proven track record of managing ship design or marine engineering projects.
  • Experience dealing with classification societies, shipyards, and marine regulatory bodies.

Skills & Competencies:

  • Strong leadership and interpersonal skills to lead cross-functional teams.
  • Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Ability to manage multiple projects with tight timelines and budgets.
  • Familiar with project planning tools (e.g., MS Project, Primavera, or equivalent).
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite.
  • High attention to detail, strong problem-solving ability, and a proactive mindset.
  • Ability to read and understand engineering drawings and technical specifications.

Desirable Attributes:

  • Familiarity with IMO, SOLAS, MARPOL, and Class rules.
  • Understanding of emerging technologies such as alternative fuels, hybrid propulsion, or digital twin systems.

postedCompany.description:
SEATECH SOLUTIONS INTERNATIONAL (S) PTE LTD


ABOUT US

Since inception in 2000, SeaTech has built a strong reputation for reliable, highly efficient and environmentally friendly vessel designs operating globally. We take pride in building a strong bond with clients, from inception, to delivery, and beyond, by listening to their operational requirements to ensure best-customised solutions. To date, SeaTech has over 300 unique designs and more than 400 vessels sailing globally.

As a centre for marine and offshore excellence, SeaTech covers the complete set of design, engineering and consulting disciplines in-house. Clients receive high quality services at cost-effective rates due to our multi-talented team using the latest modern technology.

Embracing Singapore’s “Can Do” spirit, we reach out globally but deliver locally, through regional offices in Singapore, China, India, Malaysia, Myanmar, Thailand, Japan, Vietnam, Korea, Australia and Germany.


MISSION

To Assure Optimum Performance with Design, Innovation & Technology.


VISION

A Sea of Technology
